tweets.title:
Zojila Tunnel Achieves Final Breakthrough

India’s strategically vital Zojila Tunnel has achieved its final breakthrough, bringing all-weather connectivity between Kashmir and Ladakh a major step closer.

- 13.15 km long tunnel at an altitude of 11,578 ft
- World's longest single-tube bi-directional road tunnel at this height 
- 85% of work completed, breakthrough achieved 6 months ahead of schedule
- Travel time across Zojila Pass to reduce from 1–1.5 hours to just 15 minutes 
- Expected to open by February 2028

Beyond civilian connectivity, the tunnel will significantly enhance year-round military mobility and logistics to Ladakh, eliminating the winter isolation caused by heavy snowfall at Zojila Pass.

A major boost for border infrastructure, national security, tourism, and economic development in Ladakh. #ZojilaTunnel #Ladakh #Infrastructure #IndianArmy

🇮🇳 India’s defence space is aiming a new capability leap through DefSpace Challenge 6.7.

The objective: develop a monolithic Silicon Carbide (SiC) telescope that reduces satellite payload weight by ~66% (from 600 kg to 200 kg), enabling more agile and cost-effective EO satellites.

✅ <0.5 m Panchromatic imaging for high-resolution reconnaissance
✅ 4-band Multispectral imaging at 1 m resolution
✅ 8-band SWIR imaging for camouflage detection and material identification
✅ MWIR thermal imaging for night surveillance and heat-signature tracking

Vetted by ISRO-SAC, the project aims to create a sovereign capability that does not currently exist within India’s defence space ecosystem.
